codeReview.summary:
The round-2 fix keeps the reverse chronological walk and the toolCall checkpoint semantics, so it does address the prior truncation shape at a high level. But it still reconstructs multi-text-block assistant output incorrectly by inserting `

` between blocks. The rest of the scoop stack treats an assistant turn's text as the literal concatenation of its `TextContent` blocks (`packages/webapp/src/scoops/agent-bridge.ts:599-602`, `packages/webapp/src/scoops/scoop-context.ts:489-491`), and providers such as Bedrock Camp preserve each upstream text block separately (`packages/webapp/src/providers/built-in/bedrock-camp.ts:361-367`). For a valid final assistant message like `[text('Hello'), text(' world')]`, this fix now returns `Hello

 world` instead of `Hello world`, so the original blocking issue is not fully closed for common split-block outputs. The new tests and architecture note also codify that wrong separator rather than guarding the literal-concatenation contract.

issues.description:
When the winning assistant output spans multiple text blocks, the new walker returns `collected.join('

')` instead of preserving the message's actual text content. That still breaks valid provider outputs where one assistant turn is split across adjacent text blocks: Bedrock Camp stores each upstream text block as its own `TextContent` (`packages/webapp/src/providers/built-in/bedrock-camp.ts:361-367`), while the rest of SLICC reconstructs assistant text with literal concatenation (`packages/webapp/src/scoops/agent-bridge.ts:599-602`, `packages/webapp/src/scoops/scoop-context.ts:489-491`). So `[text('Hello'), text(' world')]` now surfaces as `Hello

 world` instead of `Hello world`. The added regression tests reinforce the wrong contract by expecting injected blank lines (`packages/webapp/tests/scoops/agent-bridge.test.ts:652-689`) and never covering a split-token case like `Hello` + ` world`.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The mission library now documents the new `\n\n` joiner as the AgentBridge contract, even though the canonical assistant-text assembly paths in the codebase still use literal concatenation. If this fix is corrected, the shared architecture note should also be corrected so future workers do not inherit the wrong separator semantics.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: `.factory/library/architecture.md:39` says the collected text blocks are joined with `\n\n`; compare `packages/webapp/src/scoops/agent-bridge.ts:599-602` and `packages/webapp/src/scoops/scoop-context.ts:489-491`, both of which join assistant text blocks with `''`.

summary: Fail. The fix preserves chronology and toolCall-boundary behavior, but it still mutates split assistant text by inserting `\n\n` between adjacent text blocks, so the original multi-block output bug remains open in a different form.
